response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a strong track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and modern web stacks, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role on multiple hard requirements. Most critically, there is no indication of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a native-level requirement. His C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments and a university thesis project, with no embedded networking, LAN-switch, or Linux kernel development background. Security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) and Layer-2/3 networking experience are entirely absent from his resume.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems demonstrates strong general engineering foundation
Demonstrated low-latency systems work (DLang, async I/O, high-performance databases) shows ability to reason about performance-critical code
Linux familiarity and C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, thesis project) provide a thin but non-zero overlap with embedded development
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned — this is a non-negotiable native-level hard requirement for the role
Zero experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, Layer-2/3 switching) — the core technical domain of the position
Linux kernel development and source-code modification are required; candidate's Linux usage appears to be at the application/infrastructure layer only

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or switch security experience
